flashcards.back: A mechanism that allows a class to inherit properties and behaviors from another class, promoting code reusability.

flashcards.front: What are the five types of inheritance in C++?
flashcards.back: Single, Multiple, Multilevel, Hierarchical, and Hybrid inheritance.
An ambiguity that arises in multiple inheritance when a class inherits from two classes that both derive from the same base class.

flashcards.front: How is the diamond problem solved?
flashcards.back: Using virtual base classes to ensure only one copy of the base class exists in the derived class.

flashcards.front: What is the order of constructor execution in inheritance?
flashcards.back: From base class to most derived class (base first, then derived).

flashcards.front: What is the order of destructor execution?
flashcards.back: Reverse of constructor order (derived first, then base).

flashcards.front: What happens to private members of a base class in inheritance?
flashcards.back: They are not accessible in the derived class, regardless of inheritance type.
When multiple derived classes inherit from a single base class.
When a class is derived from another derived class, creating a chain of inheritance.
A combination of two or more types of inheritance.
